official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Construct, write, and decode the change_difficulty packet body
Define the difficulty and locked field codecs
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound change_difficulty frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns change_difficulty immediately after boss_event.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the change_difficulty id to ClientboundChangeDifficultyPacket.
The official change_difficulty body is difficulty encoded with Difficulty.STREAM_CODEC followed by locked encoded with ByteBufCodecs.BOOL.
The fixture uses official ClientboundChangeDifficultyPacket(Difficulty, boolean), so it does not require an initialized Level or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded difficulty, decoded locked flag, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove difficulty UI behavior, hardcore semantics, initialized game state, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer difficulty UI behavior, hardcore semantics, initialized game state, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
